title: US Senate GOP under pressure on Trump demand to defund NPR, PBS, foreign aid
summary: The National Public Radio headquarters in Washington, D.C., is pictured on Tuesday, May 27, 2025. (Photo by Jennifer Shutt/States Newsroom) WASHINGTON — Congress has just one week left to approve the Trump administration’s request to cancel $9.4 billion in previously approved funding for public media and foreign aid, setting up yet another tight deadline for lawmakers. The Senate must pass the bill before July 18, otherwise the White House budget office will be required to spend the funding and be barred from sending up the same proposal a...

title: ‘In the Footsteps of the Traveller’ explores the skies through the perspective of Northern Dene
summary: Amanda Frank Alaska Beacon At a book launch in May, Paul Herbert shared to a full theater at the Morris Thompson Cultural Visitors Center in Fairbanks how he met Chris Cannon, the author of the newly published book “ In the Footsteps of the Traveller: The Astronomy of Northern Dene .” One evening after he had been out on a trail all day, Herbert visited with a friend when they heard a faint knock on his door in Fort Yukon. “We looked at each other and I yelled, ‘Come in,’” Herbert said, “loud enough for someone to hear it next door.” Nothing happened and he heard another...

title: AST Boater Safety: Prince William Sound [AK25067581]
summary: On 07/05/2025 AWT contacted a skiff in Main Bay, PWS with 7 occupants on board. The captain, identified as 35 yoa Tyler Yoder from Anchorage, was not in possession of a life preserver for each person on board. Yoder was issued a boater safety citation.

title: AST Commercial Fishing: Prince William Sound [AK25067577]

title: AST Snagging: Anchorage [AK25067532]
summary: On 07/10/2025 at approximately 1630 hours, AWT contacted a 50yoa female snagging salmon in Campbell Creek. The female was identified as Debora Wani from Anchorage. Wani’s salmon was seized, and she was issued a citation for snagging in freshwater.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Sexual Assault Investigation: Fairbanks [AK25066457]
summary: This week, the Alaska State Troopers and Fairbanks Police Department arrested Gareth Ward for allegedly sexually assaulting multiple juveniles in the Fairbanks area. On July 8, 2025, at 2:29 am, Alaska State Troopers in Fairbanks received a request for a welfare check on an underage female. AST resp...
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

charges: three counts of Sexual Assault in the Second Degree related to the FPD case
entities.address_detail: in North Pole and made contact with a male subject exiting a vehicle who was identified as Gareth Ward

title: AST Domestic Violence: Talkeetna [AK25067387]
summary: On 7/10/2025 at approximately 1207 hours, the Alaska State Troopers in Palmer received a 911 open line call of a domestic incident in progress. Troopers responded to the location and identified Jonathan Hensley (48YOA) had repeatedly assaulted his significant other. Jonathan was arrested for Assault...

title: AST Sportfish Closed Period: Anchorage [AK25066890]
summary: On 07/09/2025 at approximately 0720 hours, Alaska Wildlife Troopers in Anchorage contacted Elmer Bungay, age 56 of Anchorage, while fishing in Campbell Creek near Folker Street in Anchorage. That section of the river is closed until July 14th for all sportfishing. Bungay was issued a bailable citati...

title: AST COMMERCIAL FISH CLOSED WATERS: UGASHIK BAY [AK25063807]
summary: On June 30, 2025, John C Ford 41 years old from Del Mar, California was cited by the Alaska Wildlife Troopers during the Bristol Bay Salmon Enforcement Program for Commercial Fish Closed Waters in Ugashik Bay. Arraignment is scheduled in Naknek District Court on August 6, 2025.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: Compensation program for health damage from Alaska weapons tests is extended
summary: Harlequin Beach on Amchitka Island is seen in this undated photo. The island, now part of the Alaska Maritime National Wildlife Refuge, was the site of atomic weapons tests in 1965, 1969 and 1971. (Photo provided by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service) People who might have been exposed to radiation...

summary: On July 4, 2025 Morris Lopez age 60 from Dillingham was cited by the Alaska Wildlife Troopers during the Bristol Bay Enforcement Program for negligently operating an over length drift gillnet. Arraignment is scheduled for August 5, 2025 at the Dillingham District Court.

title: AST Death Investigation: Glennallen [AK25067311]
summary: On 07/10/2025 Glennallen AST responded to an expected home death in the Glennallen area. Investigation revealed that a juvenile passed away from natural causes. Next of kin was on scene and the remains were released to the family.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: revealed that a juvenile passed away from natural causes

title: AST Assault 2, Assault 4, Criminal Mischief 5: Fairbanks [AK25067245]
summary: On 07/10//2025 at 0002 hours Troopers responded to a domestic disturbance call involving Cody Walters. An investigation was conducted, and probable cause was established to arrest Cody Walters for Assault 2, Assault 4, and Criminal Mischief 5. He was transported to FCC for remand.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: ‘Big’ bill shifts SNAP costs to states, saving federal dollars but causing fewer to get food aid
summary: A sign noting the acceptance of electronic benefit transfer (EBT) cards that are used by state welfare departments to issue benefits is displayed at a grocery store on Dec. 4, 2019, in Oakland, California. (Photo by Justin Sullivan/Getty Images) The legislative package that President Donald Trump signed into law on July 4, 2025 , has several provisions that will shrink the safety net, including the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program, long known as food stamps. SNAP spending will decline by an estimated US$186 billion through 2034 as a result of several changes Congress made to the program that today helps roughly 42 million people buy groceries – an almost 20% reduction. In my research on the history of food stamps , I’ve found that the program was meant to be widely available to most low-income people. The SNAP changes break that tradition in two ways. The Congressional Budget Office estimates that about 3 million people are likely to be dropped from the p...

title: Under bankruptcy settlement, Alaskans can request 23andMe immediately delete their DNA data
summary: A pedestrian walks by a sign posted in front of the 23andMe headquarters on Feb. 1, 2024, in Sunnyvale, California. (Photo by Justin Sullivan/Getty Images) Alaskans can request that their DNA and personal genetic information be deleted immediately by bankrupt genetic testing company 23andMe, under the terms of a recent legal settlement. State Attorney General Treg Taylor announced the option in a news release on the settlement on June 30. “ I know there are a lot of people out there who don’t realize that the samples and dat...

title: Floods are swallowing their village as EPA cuts the lifeline
summary: This article was originally published by Floodlight, a nonprofit newsroom that investigates the powers stalling climate action . Ames Alexander Floodlight Acre by acre, the village of Kipnuk is falling into the river. The small Alaskan tribal village sits on permafrost, which is thawing fast as global temperatures rise. That’s left the banks of the Kugkaktlik River unstable — and more likely to collapse when floods hit, as they often do. Buildings, boardwalks, wind turbines and other critical infrastructure are at risk, according to Rayna Paul, the village’s environmental director. So when the village learned late last ye...

title: US Education Department to revive student loan interest for borrowers in SAVE program
summary: The U.S. Education Department directed its federal student loan servicers to restart interest accrual on Aug. 1 for participants in the Biden-era SAVE plan. (Catherine Lane/Getty Images) WASHINGTON — Interest accrual on the debt of nearly 7.7 million student loan borrowers enrolled in the Saving on a Valuable Education plan will resume Aug. 1, th...
